Document:
# Survival Basics
Life in the Wasteland is hostile by default. Food, water, radiation, and weather all matter, and neglecting any of them for too long will kill you.
To survive long term, you need [color=green]food[/color] and [color=green]water[/color]. The day-night cycle lasts one hour, and during that span you need to eat and drink at least once.
## Water
Clean water is one of the most important resources in Nuclear 14. Many water sources are dirty or irradiated, so drinking whatever you find is a fast way to get sick.
Purify water with filters, chemistry, or boiling whenever you can.
## Food
You need a steady food supply. Gather wild plants, hunt animals, trade for supplies, or grow crops if your settlement has the space.
Cook raw meat before eating it, and do not assume every plant or scavenged meal is safe.
## Radiation
Radiation is everywhere in the Wasteland. Watch exposure levels, carry treatment when possible, and avoid contaminated areas unless you are equipped for them.
If a zone is hot, move quickly, wear protection, and clear the dose before it stacks up.
## Weather and Exposure
Weather can be as deadly as combat. Sandstorms, acid rain, and sharp temperature swings all punish unprepared survivors.
Keep protective clothing, a face covering, and spare supplies ready before you travel far from shelter.
